metadata.title: EMA GVP Modules III, IV, X — PV Inspections, Audits, Additional Monitoring

rules.rule_text: Competent authorities of Member States shall conduct pharmacovigilance inspections of MAHs or any firms employed to fulfil MAH pharmacovigilance obligations, in cooperation with the Agency, to determine compliance with pharmacovigilance obligations.
rules.source_quote: competent authorities of the Member States concerned shall conduct, in cooperation with the Agency, pharmacovigilance inspections of marketing authorisation holders or any firms employed to fulfil marketing authorisation holder's pharmacovigilance obligations.

rules.rule_text: Inspections shall be carried out by inspectors appointed by the national competent authorities and empowered to inspect the premises, records, documents and pharmacovigilance system master file (PSMF) of the MAH or any firms employed by the MAH.
rules.source_quote: Such inspections shall be carried out by inspectors appointed by the national competent authorities and empowered to inspect the premises, records, documents and pharmacovigilance system master file (PSMF) of the marketing authorisation holder or any firms employed by the marketing authorisation holder.

rules.rule_text: MAHs are required to provide, on request, the pharmacovigilance system master file, which will be used to inform inspection conduct.
rules.source_quote: marketing authorisation holders are required to provide, on request, the pharmacovigilance system master file, which will be used to inform inspection conduct [DIR Art 23(4) and Regulation (EC) No 726/2004 Article 16(4)].

rules.rule_text: For cause inspections shall be triggered by changes in the risk-benefit balance where further examination through an inspection is considered appropriate.
rules.source_quote: For cause pharmacovigilance inspections are undertaken when a trigger is recognised, and an inspection is considered an appropriate way to examine the issues... change in the risk-benefit balance where further examination through an inspection is considered appropriate.

rules.rule_text: For cause inspections shall be triggered by delays or failure by the MAH to identify or communicate a risk or a change in the risk-benefit balance.
rules.source_quote: delays or failure to identify or communicate a risk or a change in the risk-benefit balance.

rules.rule_text: For cause inspections shall be triggered when an MAH communicates pharmacovigilance concern information to the general public without giving prior or simultaneous notification to the national competent authorities or Agency.
rules.source_quote: communication of information on pharmacovigilance concerns to the general public without giving prior or simultaneous notification to the national competent authorities or Agency, as applicable.

rules.rule_text: For cause inspections shall be triggered by delays or omissions in reporting, poor quality or incomplete reports, or inconsistencies between reports and other information sources.
rules.source_quote: reporting obligations (expedited and periodic): delays or omissions in reporting; poor quality or incomplete reports; inconsistencies between reports and other information sources.

rules.rule_text: For cause inspections shall be triggered by concerns about the status or fulfilment of risk management plan (RMP) commitments or delays or failure to carry out specific obligations relating to the monitoring of product safety.
rules.source_quote: fulfilment of commitments: concerns about the status or fulfilment of risk management plan (RMP) commitments; delays or failure to carry out specific obligations relating to the monitoring of product safety, identified at the time of the marketing authorisation.

rules.rule_text: For cause inspections may be triggered by suspension or product withdrawal by an MAH with no advance notice to the competent authorities.
rules.source_quote: suspension or product withdrawal with no advance notice to the competent authorities.

rules.rule_text: Pre-authorisation pharmacovigilance inspections may be requested where the applicant has not previously operated a pharmacovigilance system within the EU or is in the process of establishing a new pharmacovigilance system.
rules.source_quote: the applicant has not previously operated a pharmacovigilance system within the EU or is in the process of establishing a new pharmacovigilance system.

rules.rule_text: If the outcome of a pre-authorisation inspection raises concerns, non-approval of the marketing authorisation or a re-inspection prior to approval to confirm critical findings have been addressed may be recommended.
rules.source_quote: If the outcome of the pre-authorisation inspection raises concerns about the applicant's ability to comply with the requirements laid down in the Regulation and the Directive, the following recommendations may be considered: non approval of the marketing authorisation; a re-inspection prior to approval of the marketing authorisation to confirm that critical findings and recommendations have been addressed.

rules.rule_text: Pharmacovigilance inspection planning shall be based on a systematic and risk-based approach to make the best use of surveillance and enforcement resources whilst maintaining a high level of public health protection.
rules.source_quote: Pharmacovigilance inspection planning should be based on a systematic and risk-based approach to make the best use of surveillance and enforcement resources whilst maintaining a high level of public health protection.

rules.rule_text: Inspection planning risk factors include compliance history, products with additional pharmacovigilance activities or PASS requirements, products with large patient exposure, MAHs never previously inspected, QPPV changes, and pharmacovigilance database changes.
rules.source_quote: Factors which may be taken into consideration, as appropriate, by the competent authorities when establishing pharmacovigilance inspection programmes include, but are not limited to: inspection related: compliance history identified during previous pharmacovigilance inspections... product related: product with additional pharmacovigilance activities or risk-minimisation activities... marketing authorisation holder related: marketing authorisation holder that has never been subject to a pharmacovigilance inspection.

rules.rule_text: Any party carrying out pharmacovigilance activities in whole or in part on behalf of, or in conjunction with, the MAH may be inspected. Sites to be inspected may be located inside or outside the EU.
rules.source_quote: Any party carrying out pharmacovigilance activities in whole or in part, on behalf of, or in conjunction with the marketing authorisation holder may be inspected, in order to confirm their capability to support the marketing authorisation holder's compliance with pharmacovigilance obligations. The sites to be inspected may be located in the EU... or outside the EU.

rules.rule_text: Routine pharmacovigilance inspections shall examine compliance with EU legislation and guidance and must cover ICSRs (collecting, receiving, exchanging, assessing, follow-up, reporting, record-keeping), PSURs (completeness, accuracy, timeliness), and ongoing safety evaluation.
rules.source_quote: Routine pharmacovigilance inspections conducted on behalf of the EU should examine compliance with EU legislation and guidance, and the scope of such inspections should include the following elements, as appropriate: individual case safety reports (ICSRs)... periodic safety update reports (PSURs)... ongoing safety evaluation.

rules.rule_text: Routine pharmacovigilance inspection scope shall include assessment of QPPV roles and responsibilities, accuracy and maintenance of the PSMF, quality and adequacy of staff training, computerised system fitness for purpose, and contractual arrangements.
rules.source_quote: pharmacovigilance system: QPPV roles and responsibilities, e.g. access to the quality system, the pharmacovigilance system master file, performance metrics, audit and inspection reports, and their ability to take action to improve compliance; accuracy, completeness and maintenance of the pharmacovigilance system master file; quality and adequacy of training, qualifications and experience of staff; fitness for purpose of computerised systems; contracts and agreements with all relevant parties.

rules.rule_text: Pharmacovigilance inspections shall be planned, coordinated, conducted, reported on, followed-up and documented in accordance with inspection procedures consistent with agreed Union pharmacovigilance inspection procedures developed by the PhVIWG.
rules.source_quote: Pharmacovigilance inspections should be planned, coordinated, conducted, reported on, followed-up and documented in accordance with inspection procedures consistent with agreed Union pharmacovigilance inspection procedures developed by the PhVIWG to support harmonisation for the mutual recognition of pharmacovigilance inspections within the EU.

rules.rule_text: When non-compliance is identified during an inspection, follow-up shall be required until a corrective and preventive action plan is completed. Follow-up actions include review of the CAPA plan, review of periodic progress reports, and possible re-inspection.
rules.source_quote: When non-compliance with pharmacovigilance obligations is identified during an inspection, follow-up will be required until a corrective and preventive action plan is completed. The following follow-up actions should be considered, as appropriate: review of the marketing authorisation holder's corrective and preventive action plan; review of the periodic progress reports, when deemed necessary; re-inspection to assess appropriate implementation of the corrective and preventive action plan.

rules.rule_text: If the outcome of an inspection is that the MAH does not comply with pharmacovigilance obligations, the Member State concerned shall inform the other Member States, the Agency, and the Commission.
rules.source_quote: If the outcome of the inspection is that the marketing authorisation holder does not comply with the pharmacovigilance obligations, the Member State concerned shall inform the other Member States, the Agency and the Commission in accordance with section III.C.1 [DIR Art 111(8)].

rules.rule_text: Where appropriate, the Member State concerned shall take the necessary measures to ensure that an MAH is subject to effective, proportionate and dissuasive penalties for pharmacovigilance non-compliance.
rules.source_quote: Where appropriate, the Member State concerned shall take the necessary measures to ensure that a marketing authorisation holder is subject to effective, proportionate and dissuasive penalties [DIR Art 111(8)].

rules.rule_text: Regulatory actions available following pharmacovigilance non-compliance include warning letters, non-compliance statements, Urgent Safety Restrictions, variation or suspension or revocation of the marketing authorisation, product recalls, administrative penalties, and referral for criminal prosecution.
rules.source_quote: In the event of non-compliance, possible regulatory options include the following... warning letter, non-compliance statement or infringement notice... actions against a marketing authorisation(s) or authorisation application(s) e.g. Urgent Safety Restriction; variation of the marketing authorisation; suspension or revocation of the marketing authorisation... administrative penalties, usually fixed fines or based on company profits or levied on a daily basis; referral for criminal prosecution with the possibility of imprisonment (in accordance with national legislation).

rules.rule_text: Inspectors conducting pharmacovigilance inspections shall be officials of, or appointed by, the Member State in accordance with national regulation and shall follow the provisions of the national competent authority. Training and experience shall be documented individually and evaluated.
rules.source_quote: Inspectors who are involved in the conduct of pharmacovigilance inspections requested by their Member States or by the CHMP should be officials of, or appointed by, the Member State in accordance with national regulation and follow the provisions of the national competent authority... Training and experience should be documented individually and evaluated according to the requirements of the applicable quality system of the concerned competent authority.

rules.rule_text: Inspectors shall undergo training to ensure competence in preparing, conducting and reporting inspections, and shall be kept updated with the current status of pharmacovigilance legislation and guidance.
rules.source_quote: The inspectors should undergo training to the extent necessary to ensure their competence in the skills required for preparing, conducting and reporting inspections. They should also be trained in pharmacovigilance processes and requirements... inspectors should be kept updated with the current status of pharmacovigilance legislation and guidance.

rules.rule_text: The Agency and Member States shall cooperate to share information on inspections planned and conducted, inspection scope, and inspection outcomes — particularly critical and major findings and corresponding corrective and preventive actions.
rules.source_quote: The Agency and the Member States shall cooperate to facilitate the exchange of information on inspections and in particular: information on inspections planned and conducted in order to avoid unnecessary repetition and duplication of activities in the EU and optimise the inspection resources; information on the scope of the inspection in order to focus future inspections; information on the outcome of the inspection, in particular when the outcome is that the marketing authorisation holder does not comply with the requirements.

rules.rule_text: The supervisory authority for pharmacovigilance inspections of centrally authorised products shall be the competent authority of the Member State in which the pharmacovigilance system master file is located.
rules.source_quote: According to Article 18 of Regulation (EC) 726/2004, the supervisory authority for the conduct of pharmacovigilance inspections shall be the competent authority of the Member State in which the pharmacovigilance system master file is located.

rules.rule_text: As a general approach, a marketing authorisation holder should be inspected on the basis of risk-based considerations, but at least once every 4 years.
rules.source_quote: As a general approach, a marketing authorisation holder should be inspected on the basis of risk-based considerations, but at least once every 4 years.
rules.exceptions: Risk-based approach may result in more frequent inspections.

rules.rule_text: MAHs shall always be inspection-ready as inspections may be unannounced.
rules.source_quote: always to be inspection-ready as inspections may be unannounced.

rules.rule_text: MAHs shall maintain and make available to inspectors, no later than 7 calendar days after receipt of a request, the pharmacovigilance system master file.
rules.source_quote: to maintain and make available to the inspectors on request, no later than 7 calendar days after the receipt of a request, the pharmacovigilance system master file as required by Article 23(4) of Directive 2001/83/EC and Article 16(4) of Regulation (EU) 726/2004.

rules.rule_text: MAHs shall ensure that relevant pharmacovigilance data is accessible from at least one point in the Union.
rules.source_quote: to ensure that relevant pharmacovigilance data is accessible from at least one point in the Union [DIR Art 107(1)].

rules.rule_text: MAHs shall ensure that appropriate and timely corrective and preventive action plans are implemented to address findings observed during an inspection, with appropriate prioritisation of critical and/or major findings.
rules.source_quote: to ensure that appropriate and timely corrective and preventive action plans are implemented to address findings observed during an inspection, with appropriate prioritisation of critical and/or major findings.

rules.rule_text: MAHs shall ensure that the sites selected for inspection, which may include firms employed to perform pharmacovigilance activities, agree to be inspected before the inspection is performed.
rules.source_quote: to ensure that the sites selected for inspection, which may include firms employed by the marketing authorisation holder to perform pharmacovigilance activities, agree to be inspected before the inspection is performed.

rules.rule_text: MAHs shall ensure that relevant staff involved in pharmacovigilance or related activities are present and available during the inspection for interviews or clarification of issues identified.
rules.source_quote: to ensure that relevant staff involved in pharmacovigilance activities or related activities are present and available during the inspection for interviews or clarification of issues identified.

rules.rule_text: Early re-inspection may take place where significant non-compliance has been identified and it is necessary to verify actions taken to address findings and to evaluate ongoing compliance with obligations, or where the inspected party had failed to implement corrective and preventive actions in response to an earlier inspection.
rules.source_quote: Early re-inspection may take place where significant non-compliance has been identified and where it is necessary to verify actions taken to address findings and to evaluate ongoing compliance with the obligations, including evaluation of changes in the pharmacovigilance system. Early re-inspection may also be appropriate when it is known from a previous inspection that the inspected party had failed to implement appropriately corrective and preventive actions in response to an earlier inspection.

rules.rule_text: Marketing authorisation holders in the EU are required to perform regular risk-based audits of their pharmacovigilance system, including audits of their quality system to ensure quality system requirements are met.
rules.source_quote: The marketing authorisation holder in the EU is required to perform regular risk-based audit(s) of their pharmacovigilance system [DIR Art 104(2)], including audit(s) of its quality system to ensure that the quality system complies with the quality system requirements [IR Art 8,10,11,12,13(1)].

rules.rule_text: The Agency shall perform regular independent audits of its pharmacovigilance tasks and competent authorities in Member States shall perform a regular audit of their pharmacovigilance system.
rules.source_quote: The Agency shall perform regular independent audits of its pharmacovigilance tasks [REG Art 28f] and competent authorities in Member States shall perform a regular audit of their pharmacovigilance system [DIR Art 101(2)].

rules.rule_text: The dates and results of audits and follow-up audits shall be documented.
rules.source_quote: The dates and results of audits and follow-up audits shall be documented [IR Art 13(2)].

rules.rule_text: The audit strategy shall be a high-level statement covering a period of 2-5 years and shall be endorsed by upper management. The audit programme shall be approved by upper management with overall responsibility for operational and governance structure.
rules.source_quote: The audit strategy is a high level statement of how the audit activities will be delivered over a period of time, longer than the annual programme, usually for a period of 2-5 years... An audit programme is a set of one or more audits planned for a specific timeframe, normally for a year. It should be prepared in line with the long term audit strategy. The audit programme should be approved by upper management with overall responsibility for operational and governance structure.

rules.rule_text: The risk-based audit programme shall cover the quality system for pharmacovigilance activities, critical pharmacovigilance processes, key control systems, and areas identified as high risk after controls have been put in place.
rules.source_quote: The risk-based audit programme should be based on an appropriate risk assessment and should focus on: the quality system for pharmacovigilance activities; critical pharmacovigilance processes (see e.g. GVP Module I and IR Art 11, 15); key control systems relied on for pharmacovigilance activities; areas identified as high risk, after controls have been put in place or mitigating action taken.

rules.rule_text: The audit strategy shall cover the governance, risk management and internal controls of all pharmacovigilance processes and tasks, the quality system, interactions with other departments, and pharmacovigilance activities conducted by affiliated organisations or delegated to third parties.
rules.source_quote: The audit strategy should cover the governance, risk management and internal controls of all parts of the pharmacovigilance system including: all pharmacovigilance processes and tasks; the quality system for pharmacovigilance activities; interactions and interfaces with other departments, as appropriate; pharmacovigilance activities conducted by affiliated organisations or activities delegated to another organisation.

rules.rule_text: Risk factors to be considered for audit planning include: legislative changes, major re-organisation or mergers, change in key managerial functions, significant staff turnover, significant changes to the PV system, first medicinal product on the market, and products with specific risk minimisation measures.
rules.source_quote: This is a non-prioritised, non-exhaustive list of examples of risk factors that could be considered for the purposes of a risk assessment: changes to legislation and guidance; major re-organisation or other re-structuring of the pharmacovigilance system, mergers, acquisitions... change in key managerial function(s); risk to availability of adequately trained and experienced pharmacovigilance staff... significant changes to the system since the time of a previous audit; first medicinal product on the market (for a marketing authorisation holder); medicinal product(s) on the market with specific risk minimisation measures.

rules.rule_text: The organisation shall ensure that written procedures are in place regarding the planning and conduct of individual audits, and audits shall be conducted in accordance with those written procedures in line with the GVP Module.
rules.source_quote: The organisation should ensure that written procedures are in place regarding the planning and conduct of individual audits that will be delivered. Timeframes for all the steps required for the performance of an individual audit should be settled in the relevant audit related procedures, and the organisation should ensure that audits are conducted in accordance with the written procedures, in line with this GVP Module.

rules.rule_text: Audit findings shall be documented in an audit report, communicated to management in a timely manner, and graded by relative risk level using a defined grading system with at minimum critical, major, and minor categories.
rules.source_quote: The findings of the auditors should be documented in an audit report and should be communicated to management in a timely manner... Audit findings should be reported in line with their relative risk level and should be graded in order to indicate their relative criticality to risks impacting the pharmacovigilance system, processes and parts of processes. The grading system should be defined in the description of the quality system for pharmacovigilance.

rules.rule_text: A CRITICAL audit finding is a fundamental weakness in one or more pharmacovigilance processes or practices that adversely affects the whole pharmacovigilance system and/or the rights, safety or well-being of patients, or that poses a potential risk to public health and/or represents a serious violation of applicable regulatory requirements.
rules.source_quote: critical is a fundamental weakness in one or more pharmacovigilance processes or practices that adversely affects the whole pharmacovigilance system and/or the rights, safety or well-being of patients, or that poses a potential risk to public health and/or represents a serious violation of applicable regulatory requirements.

rules.rule_text: A MAJOR audit finding is a significant weakness in one or more pharmacovigilance processes or practices, or a fundamental weakness in part of one or more pharmacovigilance processes or practices that is detrimental to the whole process and/or could potentially adversely affect the rights, safety or well-being of patients and/or could potentially pose a risk to public health and/or represents a violation of applicable regulatory requirements which is however not considered serious.
rules.source_quote: major is a significant weakness in one or more pharmacovigilance processes or practices, or a fundamental weakness in part of one or more pharmacovigilance processes or practices that is detrimental to the whole process and/or could potentially adversely affect the rights, safety or well-being of patients and/or could potentially pose a risk to public health and/or or represents a violation of applicable regulatory requirements which is however not considered serious.

rules.rule_text: A MINOR audit finding is a weakness in the part of one or more pharmacovigilance processes or practices that is not expected to adversely affect the whole pharmacovigilance system or process and/or the rights, safety or well-being of patients.
rules.source_quote: minor is a weakness in the part of one or more pharmacovigilance processes or practices that is not expected to adversely affect the whole pharmacovigilance system or process and/or the rights, safety or well-being of patients.

rules.rule_text: Issues that need to be urgently addressed shall be communicated in an expedited manner to the auditee's management and upper management. Corrective and preventive actions to address critical and major issues shall be prioritised.
rules.source_quote: Issues that need to be urgently addressed should be communicated in an expedited manner to the auditee's management and the upper management... Corrective and preventive actions to address critical and major issues should be prioritised.

rules.rule_text: Actions following an audit shall include root cause analysis and impact analysis of identified audit findings and preparation of a corrective and preventive action plan where appropriate. Evidence of completion of actions shall be recorded.
rules.source_quote: Actions should include root cause analysis and impact analysis of identified audit findings and preparation of a corrective and preventive action plan, where appropriate... Evidence of completion of actions should be recorded in order to document that issues raised during the audit have been addressed.

rules.rule_text: Capacity for follow-up audits shall be foreseen in the audit programme and shall be carried out as deemed necessary to verify the completion of agreed actions.
rules.source_quote: Capacity for follow-up audits should be foreseen in the audit programme. They should be carried out as deemed necessary, in order to verify the completion of agreed actions. [IR Art 13(2), Art 17(2)].

rules.rule_text: Pharmacovigilance audit activities shall be independent. Auditors shall be free from interference in determining the scope of auditing, performing audits, and communicating audit results. The main reporting line shall be to upper management.
rules.source_quote: Pharmacovigilance audit activities should be independent. The organisation's management should ensure this independence and objectivity in a structured manner and document this. Auditors should be free from interference in determining the scope of auditing, performing pharmacovigilance audits and communicating audit results. The main reporting line should be to the upper management with overall responsibility for operational and governance structure.

rules.rule_text: Auditors shall demonstrate and maintain proficiency in knowledge, skills and abilities covering audit principles, procedures and techniques; applicable pharmacovigilance laws and regulations; pharmacovigilance activities, processes and systems; and management and organisational systems.
rules.source_quote: Auditors should demonstrate and maintain proficiency in terms of the knowledge, skills and abilities required to effectively conduct and/or participate in pharmacovigilance audit activities... should cover knowledge, skills and abilities in: audit principles, procedures and techniques; applicable laws, regulations and other requirements relevant to pharmacovigilance; pharmacovigilance activities, processes and system(s); management system(s); organisational system(s).

rules.rule_text: When an outsourced audit service provider is used, the requirements and preparation of the audit risk assessment, strategy, programme and individual engagements shall be specified to the outsourced service provider in writing. The organisation shall obtain and document assurance of independence and objectivity of outsourced providers.
rules.source_quote: the requirements and preparation of the audit risk assessment, the audit strategy and audit programme and individual engagements should be specified to the outsourced service providers, by the organisation, in writing... the organisation should obtain and document assurance of the independence and objectivity of outsourced service providers.

rules.rule_text: The MAH shall place a note concerning critical and major audit findings of any audit relating to the pharmacovigilance system in the pharmacovigilance system master file (PSMF). An appropriate plan detailing corrective and preventive action shall be prepared and implemented.
rules.source_quote: The marketing authorisation holder shall place a note concerning critical and major audit findings of any audit relating to the pharmacovigilance system in the pharmacovigilance system master file (PSMF)... the marketing authorisation holder shall ensure that an appropriate plan detailing corrective and preventive action is prepared and implemented.

rules.rule_text: Notes concerning audit findings may be removed from the PSMF only once corrective and preventive actions have been fully implemented. Objective evidence is required in order for any note of audit findings to be removed from the PSMF.
rules.source_quote: Once the corrective and preventive actions have been fully implemented, the note may be removed [DIR Art 104(2), IR Art 13(2)]. Objective evidence is required in order that any note of audit findings can be removed from the pharmacovigilance system master file.

rules.rule_text: MAHs shall ensure that a list of all scheduled and completed audits is kept in the annex to the pharmacovigilance system master file.
rules.source_quote: The marketing authorisation holders should ensure that a list of all scheduled and completed audits is kept in the annex to the pharmacovigilance system master file (IR Art 3(5)) and that they comply with reporting commitments in line with the legislation, GVP guidance and their internal reporting policies.

rules.rule_text: Competent authorities in Member States shall report the results of their pharmacovigilance system audits to the European Commission on 21 September 2013 at the latest and then every 2 years thereafter.
rules.source_quote: Competent authorities in Member States shall report the results [of their pharmacovigilance system audits] to the Commission on 21 September 2013 at the latest and then every 2 years thereafter [DIR Art 101(2)].

rules.rule_text: The Agency shall report the results of its pharmacovigilance system audits to its Management Board on a 2-yearly basis.
rules.source_quote: The Agency shall report the results [of its pharmacovigilance system audits] to its Management Board on a 2-yearly basis [REG Art 28f].

rules.rule_text: The QPPV shall receive pharmacovigilance audit reports and provide information to auditors relevant to the risk assessment, including knowledge of status of corrective and preventive actions. The QPPV shall be notified of any audit findings relevant to the pharmacovigilance system in the EU, irrespective of where the audit was conducted.
rules.source_quote: The QPPV should receive pharmacovigilance audit reports, and provide information to the auditors relevant to the risk assessment, including knowledge of status of corrective and preventive actions. The QPPV should be notified of any audit findings relevant to the pharmacovigilance system in the EU, irrespective of where the audit was conducted.

rules.rule_text: Retention of audit reports and evidence of completion of actions shall be in line with the requirements stipulated in GVP Module I.
rules.source_quote: Retention of the audit report and evidence of completion of action needs to be in line with the requirements stipulated in GVP Module I.

rules.rule_text: All audits at competent authorities in Member States and the European Medicines Agency shall have a common ground in terms of methodology to ensure harmonised planning, implementation and reporting by every competent authority in Member States and at the Agency.
rules.source_quote: In order to have a useful audit system, all audits at the competent authorities in the Member States and the European Medicines Agency should have a common ground in terms of methodology. This should ensure harmonised planning, implementation and reporting by every competent authority in Member States and at the Agency.

rules.rule_text: It is mandatory to include medicinal products in the additional monitoring list if they contain a new active substance not contained in any medicinal product authorised in the EU on 1 January 2011.
rules.source_quote: According to Article 23(1) of Regulation (EC) No 726/2004 (REG), it is mandatory to include the following categories of medicinal products in the list: medicinal products authorised in the EU that contain a new active substance which, on 1 January 2011, was not contained in any medicinal product authorised in the EU.

rules.rule_text: It is mandatory to include any biological medicinal product not containing a new active substance (per GVPX-001) but authorised after 1 January 2011 in the additional monitoring list.
rules.source_quote: any biological medicinal product not covered by the previous category and authorised after 1 January 2011.

rules.rule_text: Products for which a PASS was requested at the time of marketing authorisation, products authorised with specific obligations on recording suspected ADRs exceeding standard requirements, products for which a PASS was requested following grant of marketing authorisation, products granted a conditional marketing authorisation, and products authorised under exceptional circumstances must mandatorily be included in the additional monitoring list.
rules.source_quote: products for which a PASS was requested at the time of marketing authorisation... products authorised with specific obligations on the recording or suspected adverse drug reactions exceeding those referred to in Chapter 3 of Directive 83/2001/EC... products for which a PASS was requested following the grant of marketing authorisation... products which were granted a conditional marketing authorisation... products authorised under exceptional circumstances.

rules.rule_text: Medicinal products subject to conditions (including conditions/restrictions on safe use, risk management system measures, post-authorisation efficacy study obligations, or requirement for an adequate PV system) may optionally be included in the additional monitoring list at the request of the European Commission or a national competent authority following PRAC consultation.
rules.source_quote: As set out in Article 23(2) of Regulation (EC) No 726/2004 there is the possibility to include in the list medicinal products subject to conditions, not falling under the mandatory scope. This can be done at the request of the European Commission or a national competent authority, as appropriate, following consultation with the Pharmacovigilance Risk Assessment Committee (PRAC).

rules.rule_text: For medicinal products containing new active substances and all biological medicinal products approved after 1 January 2011, the initial period of inclusion in the additional monitoring list is five years after the Union Reference Date (URD).
rules.source_quote: For medicinal products containing new active substances as well as for all biological medicinal products approved after 1 January 2011 the initial period of time for inclusion is five years after the Union Reference Date (URD) referred to in Article 107c(5) of Directive 2001/83/EC.

rules.rule_text: For medicinal products in the optional scope, the period of inclusion in the additional monitoring list is linked to the fulfilment of the conditions and obligations placed on the marketing authorisation and is decided by the European Commission or national competent authority.
rules.source_quote: The period of time for inclusion in the list of medicinal products authorised subject to conditions is decided by the European Commission or the national competent authority, as appropriate, is linked to the fulfilment of the conditions and obligations placed on the marketing authorisation.

rules.rule_text: MAHs shall include in the SmPC and Package Leaflet of products subject to additional monitoring the inverted equilateral black triangle symbol followed by the standardised explanatory statement: 'This medicinal product is subject to additional monitoring. This will allow quick identification of new safety information. Healthcare professionals are asked to report any suspected adverse reactions. See section 4.8 for how to report adverse reactions.'
rules.source_quote: The marketing authorisation holder: shall include in the SmPC and Package leaflet of their medicinal products subject to additional monitoring the black triangle symbol and the and the standardised explanatory statement on additional monitoring.

rules.rule_text: Once a medicinal product is included in or removed from the additional monitoring list, the MAH shall update the SmPC and package leaflet to include or remove, as appropriate, the black symbol, the statement, and the standardised explanatory statement.
rules.source_quote: Once the medicinal product is included or removed from the list, the marketing authorisation holder shall update the SmPC and the package leaflet to include or remove, as appropriate, the black symbol, the statement, and the standardised explanatory statement.

rules.rule_text: If the decision to include or remove a product from the additional monitoring list is made during assessment of a regulatory procedure, the SmPC and package leaflet shall be updated before finalisation of the procedure.
rules.source_quote: If the decision to include or remove a medicinal product from the list is done during the assessment of a regulatory procedure (e.g. marketing authorisation application, extension of indication, renewal) the SmPC and the package leaflet should be updated before finalisation of the procedure in order to include or remove the black triangle symbol and explanatory statement from the product information.

rules.rule_text: If the decision to include or remove a medicinal product from the additional monitoring list is made outside a regulatory procedure, the MAH shall subsequently submit a variation to update the product information accordingly.
rules.source_quote: If the decision to include or remove a medicinal product from the list is done outside a regulatory procedure, then the marketing authorisation holder is requested to subsequently submit a variation to update the product information of that product accordingly.

rules.rule_text: MAHs should include information on the status of additional monitoring in any material distributed to healthcare professionals and patients and should make all efforts to encourage reporting of adverse reactions, as agreed with national competent authorities.
rules.source_quote: should include information on the status of additional monitoring in any material to be distributed to healthcare professionals and patients and should make all efforts to encourage reporting of adverse reactions, as agreed with national competent authorities.

rules.rule_text: The Agency shall publish and keep up to date on the European web-portal the list of medicinal products subject to additional monitoring, including electronic links to product information and the summary of the RMP.
rules.source_quote: is responsible for publishing the list of medicinal products that are subject to additional monitoring on the European web-portal with an electronic link(s) to a webpage where the product information and the summary of the RMP are publicly available.

rules.rule_text: The additional monitoring list shall be updated monthly following each PRAC meeting, as appropriate.
rules.source_quote: The list will be updated monthly following each PRAC meeting, as appropriate.

rules.rule_text: National competent authorities shall notify the Agency, within 15 days of granting the marketing authorisation nationally, of products subject to additional monitoring under mandatory scope, and provide electronic links to national web-portals where product information and the RMP summary are publicly available.
rules.source_quote: each national competent authority included in such procedures should inform the Agency, within 15 days of granting the marketing authorisation nationally, and provide the electronic links to their national web-portal where the product information and the summary of the RMP are publicly available.

rules.rule_text: For centrally authorised products in the optional scope, the Agency shall include the product in the additional monitoring list within 15 days of receipt of the European Commission decision.
rules.source_quote: The Agency will include centrally authorised products in the list within 15 days of receipt of the European Commission decision.

rules.rule_text: National competent authorities shall make publicly available on their national web-portal the list of medicinal products authorised in their territory that are subject to additional monitoring, including an electronic link to product information and the RMP summary.
rules.source_quote: make publicly available in their national web-portal the list of medicinal products authorised in their territory that are subject to additional monitoring. The list shall include an electronic link to a webpage where the product information and the summary of the RMP are publicly available.

rules.rule_text: The Agency and national competent authorities shall take into account the list of medicinal products subject to additional monitoring in determining the frequency and processes of their signal detection activities.
rules.source_quote: will take into account the list of centralised medicinal products subject to additional monitoring in determining the frequency and processes of its signal detection activities... take into account the list of nationally authorised medicinal products subject to additional monitoring in determining the frequency and processes of their signal detection activities.

rules.rule_text: The Agency and national competent authorities shall inform the relevant MAH when a medicinal product has been included in the list of additional monitored products.
rules.source_quote: will inform the relevant MAH when a centralised medicinal product has been included to the list of additional monitored products... inform the relevant MAH when a nationally medicinal product has been included to the list of additional monitored products.

rules.rule_text: MAHs should provide evidence to the competent authorities concerned on the status of any conditions imposed by the national competent authorities or the European Commission relating to additional monitoring status.
rules.source_quote: should provide evidence to the competent authorities concerned on the status of any conditions imposed by the national competent authorities or the European Commission.

rules.rule_text: The Agency shall make publicly available the list of names and active substances of all medicinal products approved in the EU subject to additional monitoring and the general criteria used to include medicinal products in the list.
rules.source_quote: Pursuant to Article 23 of Regulation 726/2004, the Agency will make publicly available the list of the names and active substances of all medicinal products approved in the EU subject to additional monitoring and the general criteria to include medicinal products in the list.
